---
title: "LRPDID13: treatment-effect heterogeneity — word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** Do children differ in how much the programme helped their word reading,
or is the effect much the same for everyone? This model estimates the between-child spread of
the crossover treatment effect directly, rather than sorting children into "responders" and
"non-responders" (which invents groups out of noise).

LRPDID13 is the word-reading waitlist-crossover DiD (as LRPDID01), extended with a **per-child
random slope on the on-intervention indicator**: the crossover effect for child $i$ is
$\delta_i = \delta + \sigma_\delta\,z_i$ (non-centred). `delta` is the population-mean effect,
read exactly as in the other DiD models; **`sigma_delta` — the between-child SD of the effect
on the logit scale — is the estimand of record here**.

::: {.callout-note title="The honest non-responder ladder (#230 §4a)"}

This is rung 1 of the non-responder question: _does child-level response variance exist at
all?_ If `sigma_delta` concentrates near zero, apparent non-response is noise plus the
covariates already modelled, and we do **not** gate-keep on early response. Only if real
variance exists is rung 2 — a one-stage moderation with shrinkage on a **pre-randomisation**
moderator — warranted; never a two-stage classify-then-compare, which manufactures a subgroup
from noise (Senn 2016, doi:10.1002/sim.7059). The `P(sigma_delta < 0.1)` figure below, read
against the prior mass below the same threshold, says whether the data moved the estimate. See
`METHODS.md`.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \delta_i\,\text{Treated}_{i,p}
           + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i},
\qquad
\delta_i = \delta + \sigma_\delta\,z_i,\; z_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1),
$$

with a Beta-Binomial likelihood on the period post-count ($N = 79$) and no own-baseline term
(the immediate arm's period-start score is treatment-affected). `Treated` is 1 when the child
is receiving the intervention that period (immediate: both; waitlist: P2 only). A correlated
intercept–slope (LKJ) form is deferred as a prior-dominated sensitivity at $n \approx 54$.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}

## Treatment-effect heterogeneity

::: {.callout-warning title="What `sigma_delta` separates — and what it conflates"}
`sigma_delta` is identified almost entirely from the **waitlist crossers**, each contributing
a single within-child P1→P2 difference. The immediate arm is `Treated = 1` in _both_ periods,
so for those children the random intercept $u_i$ and the random slope $v_i$ enter only as their
sum and cannot be separated within-child; with independent `HalfNormal(0.5)` priors on
`sigma_child` and `sigma_delta`, the immediate arm's level heterogeneity is partitioned between
the two SDs by the prior rather than the data. Read `sigma_delta` as between-child variation in
the **waitlist-crossover** effect that, for the immediate arm, is not cleanly separable from
intercept heterogeneity — the `P(sigma_delta < 0.1)`-vs-prior comparison below is the honesty
check, and the correlated intercept–slope (LKJ) form is the deferred sensitivity that would
address the conflation directly.
:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
_het = _csv("heterogeneity_summary.csv")
if _het is None:
    print("_No heterogeneity summary found for this model._\n")
else:
    _r = _het.iloc[0]
    _cols = list(_het.columns)
    print("| " + " | ".join(_cols) + " |")
    print("| " + " | ".join("---" for _ in _cols) + " |")
    print("| " + " | ".join(f"{_r[c]:.3f}" if isinstance(_r[c], float) else str(_r[c]) for c in _cols) + " |")
    print(
        "\n\n`sigma_delta` is the between-child SD of the on-intervention effect on the "
        "logit scale. Compare `P(sigma_delta < 0.1)` against `prior_P(sigma_delta < 0.1)`: "
        "if the posterior mass below the threshold is much larger than the prior's, the data "
        "point toward a homogeneous effect (little reliable between-child variation); if it is "
        "similar to the prior, the data are uninformative about heterogeneity at this sample "
        "size. Either way, this is the honest basis for the non-responder discussion — not a "
        "responder/non-responder split.\n"
    )
```

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
